sheet.iconAutoDescription: When empty, the icon is inferred from the model name first. Current vendor category: {vendor}.
sheet.descriptionPlaceholder: Model purpose, limits, or display notes

sheet.systemPromptDescription: Applies only to chat tasks. If the model does not support native system prompts, it is merged into the user prompt.
sheet.capabilitiesDescription: Configure model default parameters and visual controls in the chat parameters panel.

capabilitiesPreset.title: Quickly reuse model capabilities
capabilitiesPreset.description: Choose a protocol template, or copy capabilities from an existing model.
capabilitiesPreset.modelSearchPlaceholder: Search reusable configs with the same protocol and type

capabilitiesQuick.title: Model capabilities config
capabilitiesQuick.description: Configure model parameters and allowed official native tools.
capabilitiesQuick.invalidJSON: The current model capabilities JSON is invalid. Fix it before using visual config.
capabilitiesQuick.parametersIntro: Configure user-visible model parameters. A default value is also used as that parameter's request default.
capabilitiesQuick.toolsIntro: Configure official native tools allowed for this model. Only tools enabled here can enter conversation settings.
capabilitiesQuick.defaultsHelp: Default parameters are written to defaultOptions. value is parsed as a JSON value, for example 0.7, true, null, "high", or an object.
capabilitiesQuick.controlsHelp: Parameter controls are written to optionControls. path maps to the real request option path, and final passthrough is still controlled by the option policy.
capabilitiesQuick.toolsHelp: Official native tools are written to nativeTools. One tool can target multiple protocols; default-enabled tools are added to the user's default options automatically. User JSON tools are preserved by the backend only when they match these official tools.
capabilitiesQuick.validationFailed: Complete the highlighted configuration fields first.
capabilitiesQuick.nativeToolProtocolRequired: Enter at least one applicable protocol
capabilitiesQuick.nativeToolTypeRequired: Enter a type or provide type in payload
capabilitiesQuick.nativeToolPayloadInvalid: Payload must be a JSON object
capabilitiesQuick.nativeToolProtocolWarning: Current route protocol is not adapted; force enabling will pass through the administrator configuration.
capabilitiesQuick.incompatibleNativeToolsHelp: The native tools below may not apply to the current route protocol. Enable them carefully.

capabilitiesGuide.title: Model capabilities JSON guide
capabilitiesGuide.description: This JSON stores model defaults, visual option controls, and official native tool capabilities. It is normalized against the current route protocol on save.
capabilitiesGuide.defaultsDescription: defaultOptions becomes this model's default request parameters. The chat page uses it when the user has no saved local parameters for the model; backend option policy still applies before sending.
capabilitiesGuide.controlsDescription: optionControls only defines how the chat parameters dialog renders controls. It is not sent to the model by itself. path maps to the real options path.
capabilitiesGuide.toolsDescription: nativeTools defines the provider-hosted official tools allowed for this model. Each tool is configured once, and protocols lists the applicable request protocols; a multi-protocol tool does not need duplicate rows.
capabilitiesGuide.toolsAutoDescription: When users hand-write options.tools, only admin-allowed official tools are preserved. Parameters on allowed tools pass through; unknown tools and MCP/function tools cannot be injected through JSON.
capabilitiesGuide.controlTypes: type supports text, select, number, and boolean. select controls should provide options.
capabilitiesGuide.policyDescription: Rendering a control does not guarantee passthrough. The same path still needs to be allowed by the option policy; the backend filters options before sending.
